title: Illinois signs an AI safety law with audits and incident reporting
friendlyTitle: Illinois creates new safety rules for powerful AI systems
summary: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker signed SB 315, the Artificial Intelligence Safety Measures Act, creating state-level requirements for covered advanced AI developers to disclose safety practices, report significant incidents, maintain compliance processes, and undergo independent third-party safety audits. The law also includes confidential reporting channels and whistleblower protections.
friendlySummary: Illinois passed a law requiring some large AI developers to share safety practices, report serious incidents, and get outside audits.
whyItMatters: AI governance is becoming a patchwork of state and national rules that businesses will need to track. Audit and incident-reporting requirements may push frontier model developers toward more formal safety and compliance operations.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could affect how AI companies build, test, and explain their most powerful systems.
